title: Embodied regulation and state-sensitive functioning
description: Reviewed evidence supports anticipatory and reactive regulation and condition-sensitive functioning; physiological signals do not transparently disclose subjective state or fixed capacity.
scope: General human baseline with adolescent and education-context modifier
limitations: Component evidence uses different populations and constructs; causal size and developmental specificity remain uncertain.
counterevidence: Biomarkers are non-specific and educational outcome effects are heterogeneous.
related_question: How do embodied conditions affect action and observed functioning?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer motive potential prognosis or fixed circuitry from a state signal or performance.
revision_trigger: Formal review finds false convergence or causal overreach.
notes: Classification: partial thematic convergence; mechanisms and populations differ; inferential independence unknown beyond direct-duplicate audit.

title: Bounded cognition and action
description: Attention information and computational capacity are limited; prior knowledge changes effective processing demand and transfer beyond familiar contexts is not automatic.
scope: General-human component finding plus education-context modifier; not convergence-tested at mechanism level
limitations: General boundedness does not establish a universal capacity limit or transport of particular strategies.
counterevidence: Heuristics and social learning can preserve either effective action or error; laboratory retention is not authentic far transfer.
related_question: How do cognitive limits and knowledge interact?
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate recall with understanding or transfer or create a universal bias inventory.
revision_trigger: Formal review finds construct mismatch or unsupported generalisation.
notes: Classification: component-specific layers; underlying review/sample overlap not assessed.

title: Agency and motivational quality
description: Reviewed literatures distinguish control beliefs available means opportunities habits planning and motivational qualities; no single trait or causal agency architecture is established.
scope: Two-layer synthesis of component-specific constructs; convergence limited to caution against trait/compliance inference
limitations: Western-developed autonomy and authorship constructs and one shared reward source reduce independence.
counterevidence: Persistence can be maladaptive; habits can conflict with goals; visible effort can arise from pressure as well as endorsement.
related_question: What can observed action reveal about agency and motive?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer agency endorsement or responsibility from confidence persistence choice or compliance.
revision_trigger: Construct-equivalent cross-cultural evidence or review shows integration is misleading.
notes: Western-developed constructs; partly source-dependent through S1-SRC-009 / S1B-W2-SRC-006.

title: Affiliation and boundaries
description: Across represented settings social networks commonly include overlapping kin and non-kin ties; categories can alter expectations without inherently producing hostility. Conditions linking affiliation with care indifference exclusion or hostility remain unresolved.
scope: Partial thematic convergence; mechanisms and populations differ
limitations: Western belonging measures and sparse field-site coverage limit universality; school-based evidence misses disconnected populations.
counterevidence: Category effects vary with norms identification and task meaning; contact and cross-cutting identities do not mechanically dissolve boundaries.
related_question: How do affiliation and boundaries shape social action?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer inevitable tribalism a natural group unit or belonging from proximity or popularity.
revision_trigger: Formal review identifies unsupported cross-setting generalisation.
notes: Direct duplication checked; inferential independence unknown; care/exclusion linked to S1R-UNC-004 rather than resolved.

title: Plural rank and influence
description: Reviewed evidence distinguishes prestige dominance authority inherited position and resource control; advantage can reinforce itself under some conditions and can be resisted.
scope: Stage 1B component-specific general baseline with separate Stage 1A peer-status modifier
limitations: Evidence is uneven across societies genders and public versus domestic domains; the full feedback loop is not jointly identified.
counterevidence: Rank can be domain-specific and unstable; esteem need not become command or resource control; apparent egalitarianism may conceal inequality.
related_question: How do status and power recur without becoming inevitable?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat hierarchy as unitary innate inevitable meritorious or legitimate.
revision_trigger: Broader gendered and cross-cultural evidence changes the distinctions or feedback account.
notes: Not cross-component convergence; evidence coverage is uneven across societies genders and visible domains.

title: Conditional collective action
description: Observed cooperation and punishment outcomes vary with expectations reciprocity boundaries rules sanction structure power and retaliation.
scope: Component-specific general human baseline from Stage 1B
limitations: Evidence is weighted toward games and selected cases and does not establish durable institutional causation.
counterevidence: Punishment can target contributors reduce welfare or provoke retaliation and is neither necessary nor sufficient for cooperation.
related_question: What conditions accompany cooperation and enforcement?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer justice legitimacy benevolence selfishness or one institutional recipe.
revision_trigger: Field evidence materially changes conditional outcome patterns.
notes: Classification: component-specific general human baseline; Stage 1A offers no passed substantive counterpart.

title: Meaning commitment and coercion
description: Western meaning research distinguishes coherence direction or purpose and mattering; separate evidence links ritual protected values and contribution conditionally with commitment-related outcomes.
limitations: Meaning measures are Western-heavy; ritual studies face selection; contribution may be coerced; evidence bases do not establish one mechanism.
counterevidence: Role fulfilment may organise meaning differently; ritual can accompany exclusion and protected values can entrench conflict.
related_question: How are commitments made meaningful and when are they coerced?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer universal purpose sacred values necessary ritual beneficial sacrifice or voluntary contribution.
revision_trigger: Construct-equivalent cross-cultural or coercion-sensitive evidence changes the claims.
notes: Classification: component-specific and partly unresolved; compliance/endorsement overlap is not source convergence.

title: Measurement and structured variation
description: Measurement design translation and construct equivalence affect which differences become visible and comparable; population patterns are not complete individual profiles.
scope: Directly source-dependent convergence on a measurement caution; substantive transportability unresolved
limitations: Shared sources and repeated constructs reduce independence; measurement failure does not eliminate real variation.
counterevidence: Some constructs show partial invariance and useful bounded comparisons; sampling critique does not invalidate every finding.
related_question: How should variation and measurement constrain inference?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat measured difference as essence non-invariance as no variation or a group average as an individual future.
revision_trigger: Improved equivalent measures or review identifies incompatible constructs.
notes: Five DOI-identical records; overlap among primary samples inside reviews not assessed.

title: Adolescent control and social salience
description: Adolescents possess meaningful control while reliability of deployment and goal priorities may vary with development task emotion and social context; no single maturity timetable is established.
limitations: Stage 1B excluded adolescence; Stage 1A evidence is Western-heavy and task-limited.
counterevidence: Age puberty sensation seeking and risk do not follow one curve; laboratory tasks are imperfect proxies for everyday action.
related_question: What adolescent modifiers must the baseline preserve?
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer irrationality recklessness maturity or incapacity from age puberty or one task.
revision_trigger: New component review materially changes adolescent developmental constraints.
notes: Classification: adolescent/education-context modifier; no Stage 1B convergence test.

title: Educational observation is incomplete evidence
description: An isolated educational performance can reflect interactions among knowledge processing demands motive bodily state development opportunity and context but cannot identify their contribution in an individual case.
limitations: The synthesis cannot diagnose hidden mechanisms and must not become context determinism.
counterevidence: A weak performance may reflect a real knowledge or skill gap; stable individual differences and agency remain possible.
related_question: What may be inferred from observed educational performance?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat performance as a complete reading of person or context as an automatic explanation or excuse.
revision_trigger: Formal review finds the interaction synthesis unfalsifiable or stronger than component records.
notes: Classification: adolescent/education-context modifier with a convergent general inference caution.

title: Peer and school-belonging modifier
description: Peer contexts can amplify prosocial or risky local norms; mainly school-based belonging evidence concerns recognition accepted identity and safety while popularity acceptance friendship and identification remain distinct.
limitations: Selection and influence are difficult to separate; belonging evidence is correlational and omits many disconnected learners.
counterevidence: Group attachment can support participation or sharpen exclusion; popularity can coexist with low acceptance.
related_question: How do peer audiences and institutions modify affiliation?
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat peers as uniformly harmful or belonging as causally sufficient or a safeguarding substitute.
revision_trigger: Better causal or missing-population evidence changes the modifier.

title: Two-layer integration frame
description: Stage 1R provisionally separates general human patterns from adolescent and educational-expression modifiers.
relevance: Required to reconcile differently scoped component studies
limitations: The layers may overlap or conceal mechanisms and are not natural modules.
counterevidence: Alternatives include discipline-based or mechanism-based organisation.
related_question: How should component findings be organised?
prohibited_overextension: Do not present the two layers as a discovered human architecture.
revision_trigger: Review shows the frame creates false convergence or unusable separation.
notes: If wrong Stage 1R must regroup claims before passing.

title: Recurrence inference rule
description: Recurrence is compatible with preparedness common demands learning diffusion institutions and artefact and does not itself establish innateness.
limitations: The rule does not rank alternatives or prove biology absent.
counterevidence: Discriminating developmental comparative and mechanistic evidence could support stronger bounded origin claims.
prohibited_overextension: Do not reverse this into cultural determinism.
revision_trigger: Discriminating evidence establishes a bounded origin account.
notes: If wrong the project may understate genuine preparedness.

title: Component reviews are sufficient for reconciliation
description: Reviewed component pass-forwards and linked ledgers are sufficient to draft a provisional reconciliation without new evidence.
limitations: Component omissions and differing inclusion strategies may leave gaps.
counterevidence: Return unresolved gaps to Stage 1A or Stage 1B rather than filling them here.
prohibited_overextension: Do not interpret absence from a component as disproof.
revision_trigger: Formal review identifies a material claim that cannot be reconciled from authorised records.
notes: If wrong Stage 1R must request component revision.

title: Stage boundary against normative and design derivation
description: Human-baseline findings constrain but do not define flourishing responsibility justice education purpose capabilities or curriculum.
relevance: Required to preserve the research chain
limitations: Later stages may seek answers this baseline cannot supply.
counterevidence: Stage 2 performs explicit normative analysis and later stages derive design.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat baseline regularities as educational aims.
revision_trigger: Operating system or authorised stage dependency changes.
notes: Binding stage/dependency decision under the operating system.

title: Coordination is not equivalent to justice
description: Stage 2 must not infer legitimacy or justice solely from cooperation stability contribution punishment ritual or rank.
limitations: The judgement does not determine a complete theory of justice.
counterevidence: Some consequentialist views may treat welfare or contribution as sufficient proxies.
prohibited_overextension: Do not present the boundary as an empirical finding.
revision_trigger: Stage 2 adopts and justifies an alternative evaluative framework.
notes: Value basis: separate coordination from legitimacy; trade-off: refuses one outcome metric; disagreement is expected.

title: Power and refusal must remain visible
description: When Stage 2 evaluates rank duty contribution or institutions it should identify beneficiaries costs power and whether refusal is credible.
limitations: A power-first lens can underweight valued coordination or non-power explanations.
counterevidence: Participants may endorse asymmetry or shared obligation and analysts may dispute the lens.
related_question: What costs and power relations must remain visible?
prohibited_overextension: Do not assume every asymmetry is illegitimate.
revision_trigger: Formal review finds the protocol distorts rather than clarifies Stage 2 reasoning.
notes: Value basis and trade-off are explicit; not a human-baseline fact.

title: Cultural transportability
description: Which claims survive broader linguistic ecological institutional class and gender sampling with equivalent constructs?
limitations: Both corpora remain culturally uneven.
uncertainty_description: Cross-cultural transportability and construct equivalence remain unresolved.
prohibited_overextension: Do not resolve through country count or rhetorical convergence.
revision_trigger: New construct-equivalent evidence changes a claim.

title: Origin and recurrence
description: How can evidence distinguish preparedness common demands learning diffusion institutions and artefact?
limitations: Present evidence underdetermines origin.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer innateness adaptation or absence of biology.
revision_trigger: Discriminating developmental comparative and mechanistic evidence emerges.

title: Agency pathways
description: How do autonomy competence prior success opportunity relationships habits and deliberate control interact?
limitations: Constructs are heterogeneous and causal identification is limited.
prohibited_overextension: Do not install one agency model through synthesis.
revision_trigger: Causal cross-cultural evidence identifies pathways.

title: Affiliation direction
description: What determines whether affiliation and boundaries support care indifference exclusion or hostility?
limitations: Selection causation local norms and power remain difficult to separate.
uncertainty_description: Belonging mechanisms and affiliation direction remain unresolved.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat belonging or categorisation as causally sufficient.
revision_trigger: Causal and culturally broad evidence resolves mechanisms.

title: Status conversion and hidden power
description: When does esteem convert into dominance authority inheritance or resource control and how do gendered domestic and less visible systems alter the account?
limitations: Public and male-visible evidence is overrepresented.
related_question: How does rank convert and remain hidden?
uncertainty_description: Status conversion and hidden systems remain unresolved.
prohibited_overextension: Do not presume rank evidence is gender-neutral or exhaustive.
revision_trigger: Broader evidence changes rank mechanisms or visibility.

title: Cooperation versus extraction
description: When do monitoring rules and sanctions sustain mutual benefit rather than protect extraction or retaliation?
limitations: Game evidence and selected cases do not determine durable legitimacy.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer justice from contribution or stability.
revision_trigger: Durable field evidence clarifies power and outcomes.

title: Meaning endorsement and coercion
description: How can evidence distinguish endorsed meaning and contribution from obligation under dependence or coercion?
limitations: Western meaning measures and unclear voluntariness limit inference.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer responsibility purpose or flourishing from compliance or contribution.
revision_trigger: Cross-cultural coercion-sensitive evidence changes inference.

title: Adolescent and educational specificity
description: How specific are state social and control effects to KS3-KS4 and where are developmental boundaries?
limitations: Age puberty context and task measures are non-equivalent.
uncertainty_description: Sensitive periods and embodied developmental specificity remain unresolved.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer one maturity timetable.
revision_trigger: Stronger longitudinal developmental evidence changes boundaries.

title: Transfer and observation
description: How should later work distinguish knowledge recognition motivation and deployment without diagnosing from one performance?
limitations: Current evidence cannot partition mechanisms in an individual case.
uncertainty_description: Authentic transfer and mechanism attribution remain unresolved.
prohibited_overextension: Do not turn interpretive caution into hidden diagnosis.
revision_trigger: Valid repeated and context-sensitive evidence clarifies inference.

title: Intersectional variation
description: How do disability neurotype language gender poverty culture and institutional access interact?
limitations: Existing component evidence does not support a complete interaction model.
bias_or_access_issue: disability language gender poverty and access
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat categories as complete profiles or independent variables.
revision_trigger: Intersectional and accessible evidence materially changes claims.

title: False convergence
description: Shared language or overlapping constructs may be mistaken for independent corroboration.
limitations: At least five DOI-identical records were identified; overlap among underlying studies or samples inside reviews was not assessed.
prohibited_overextension: Do not raise confidence merely because both components use similar terms.
revision_trigger: Audit identifies additional shared samples or constructs.

title: Normative leakage
description: Empirical regularities may be converted into flourishing responsibility justice or education-purpose claims.
limitations: Stage 1 records do not settle evaluative questions.
prohibited_overextension: Do not pass normative conclusions as human facts.
revision_trigger: Draft handoff contains a normative claim disguised as empirical.

title: Deterministic inference
description: General or group patterns may be used to label individual motive capacity maturity or potential.
limitations: Measurement and context cautions do not eliminate real individual differences.
bias_or_access_issue: disability neurotype demographic and cultural bias
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer an individual future from a group pattern or one performance.
revision_trigger: Review finds insufficient safeguards.

title: Hierarchy and functionalist legitimation
description: Recurrence coordination or stability may be treated as necessity merit benefit or legitimacy.
limitations: A power lens can also overcorrect and obscure valued coordination.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer ought from recurrence or coordination.
revision_trigger: Formal review finds naturalising or one-sided language.

title: Causal inflation
description: Associations and analytical frames may be rewritten as causal architectures.
limitations: Component reviews contain heterogeneous observational and experimental evidence.
prohibited_overextension: Do not state that culture institutions or context determine action.
revision_trigger: Evidence audit identifies causal wording beyond component support.

title: Premature downstream authority
description: Draft reconciliation may be treated as authorising Stage 2 before review.
limitations: Both components explicitly block independent downstream use.
prohibited_overextension: Do not use draft findings downstream until formal Stage 1R gate passes.
revision_trigger: PASS_FORWARD status changes without a recorded pass decision.

title: Use only reviewed component interfaces
description: Reconciliation compares Stage 1A PF-01 through PF-07 and Stage 1B 1B-PF-01 through 1B-PF-13 plus linked ledger records.
limitations: May omit useful unpassed narrative material by design.
prohibited_overextension: Do not use uncited report prose as new evidence.
revision_trigger: Formal review finds a component record was used outside its pass-forward authority.

title: Conservative confidence ceiling
description: Merged findings default to Moderate unless a narrower caution is independently robust; convergence does not automatically increase confidence.
limitations: May understate some well-supported component findings.
prohibited_overextension: Do not average or add confidence labels.
revision_trigger: Evidence audit supports a different bounded confidence.

title: Preserve component-specific claims
description: Absence from one component is not contradiction; cooperation meaning adolescence and transfer remain component-specific where appropriate.
limitations: Component boundaries may leave important cross-domain links untested.
prohibited_overextension: Do not invent convergence from silence.
revision_trigger: Review identifies direct component contradiction or overlooked passed counterpart.

title: Keep Stage 2 blocked
description: The draft handoff remains unauthorised until evidence claim bias and adversarial reviews are complete.
limitations: Delays Stage 2 but prevents authority drift.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat review status as pass.
revision_trigger: Formal review records pass or pass_with_cautions.

title: Embodiment trace
description: Maps component regulation and state findings to reconciled claim S1R-CLM-001.
limitations: No new evidentiary weight is created.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat mapping as evidence.
revision_trigger: Any linked component claim is revised.

title: Cognition and agency trace
description: Maps bounded cognition control motivation knowledge and transfer records to S1R-CLM-002 and S1R-CLM-003.
limitations: Constructs overlap but are not identical.
related_question: How do cognition and agency merge?
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim one causal architecture.
revision_trigger: Any linked component claim is revised.

title: Affiliation and status trace
description: Maps affiliation belonging boundaries and status records to S1R-CLM-004 and S1R-CLM-005.
limitations: General and school-specific constructs remain separated.
prohibited_overextension: Do not collapse belonging into status or affiliation into tribalism.
revision_trigger: Any linked component claim is revised.

title: Collective action and meaning trace
description: Maps Stage 1B cooperation enforcement meaning and commitment records to S1R-CLM-006 and S1R-CLM-007.
limitations: These are component-specific rather than convergent findings.
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim Stage 1A corroboration.
revision_trigger: Any linked component claim is revised.

title: Variation trace
description: Maps measurement culture opportunity and learner-variation records to S1R-CLM-008.
limitations: Shared sources reduce independence.
prohibited_overextension: Do not double-count shared sources.
revision_trigger: Source-independence audit changes.

title: Adolescent and education modifier trace
description: Maps adolescent performance peer and standard-learner records to S1R-CLM-009 through S1R-CLM-011.
limitations: Stage 1B did not test adolescence or educational expression.
prohibited_overextension: Do not generalise modifiers to all humans.
revision_trigger: Any linked component claim is revised.
